description.en:
Adjust the working speed limits of your implements on the fly while driving. Push past default limits or fine-tune for precision work.
Note: This is a proof of concept experiment. Features and keybindings may change. Singleplayer only. Speed adjustments reset on map reload.
Features: - Increase or decrease implement working speed in 1 km/h steps (RightShift+1/2) - Reset speed and antigravity to defaults (RightShift+3) - Toggle antigravity mode to bypass physics-based speed penalties (RightShift+0) - Status line in F1 help panel shows current working speed and antigravity state - Respects player's unit preference (km/h or mph) - Keybindings appear automatically when vehicle has speed-limited implements attached - Works while AI worker is driving - Adjusts all attached implements simultaneously
Limitations: - Only works on towed/attached implements, not self-propelled work vehicles - Speed adjustments reset on map reload - Other mods (e.g. Courseplay) may set their own speed limits that override adjustments - Singleplayer only
Usage: Enter a vehicle with implements attached. Keybindings appear in the F1 help menu. RightShift+1: Increase working speed RightShift+2: Decrease working speed RightShift+3: Reset speed and antigravity to defaults RightShift+0: Toggle antigravity mode
Source code, documentation, and issue tracker: https://github.com/rittermod/FS25_AdjustImplementSpeed
Changelog: 0.0.2.0 (Experiment): - Added reset keybind (RightShift+3) to restore default working speed and disable antigravity - Fixed speed display to respect player's unit preference (km/h or mph)
0.0.1.0 (Experiment): - Added dynamic working speed adjustment for attached implements (RightShift+1/2) - Added antigravity mode to bypass physics-based speed penalties (RightShift+0) - Added F1 help panel status line showing current working speed and antigravity state - Keybindings appear automatically when vehicle has speed-limited implements attached - Works while AI worker is driving
